contentBlocks.original:
कृपासागरायाशुकाव्यप्रदाय
प्रणम्राखिलाभीष्टसन्दायकाय ।
यतीन्द्रैरुपास्याङ्घ्रिपाथोरुहाय
प्रबोधप्रदात्रे नमः शङ्कराय ॥1॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛpāsāgarāyāśukāvyapradāya
praṇamrākhilābhīṣṭasandāyakāya ।
yatīndrairupāsyāṅghripāthōruhāya
prabōdhapradātrē namaḥ śaṅkarāya ॥1॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Śiva — who is an ocean of compassion, who quickly bestows the gift of eloquent poetry, who grants all the desired objects of those who bow before him, whose lotus-feet are worshipped by the foremost among ascetics (yatīndras), and who is the giver of supreme awakening (prabodha).
contentBlocks.meanings: kṛpā-sāgara: ocean of compassion | āśu-kāvya-pradā: quick giver of poetry/eloquence | praṇamra: one who bows | akhila-abhīṣṭa-sandāyaka: granter of all desired things | yatīndra: foremost ascetic | upāsya-aṅghri-pātho-ruha: whose lotus-feet are worshipped | prabodha-pradātṛ: giver of awakening | śiva: Śiva

contentBlocks.original:
चिदानन्दरूपाय चिन्मुद्रिकोद्य-
त्करायेशपर्यायरूपाय तुभ्यम् ।
मुदा गीयमानाय वेदोत्तमाङ्गैः
श्रितानन्ददात्रे नमः शङ्कराय ॥2॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chidānandarūpāya chinmudrikōdya-
tkarāyēśaparyāyarūpāya tubhyam ।
mudā gīyamānāya vēdōttamāṅgaiḥ
śritānandadātrē namaḥ śaṅkarāya ॥2॥
contentBlocks.translation: To you — who are of the nature of consciousness-bliss (cid-ānanda), whose hand is upraised in the cin-mudrā (the gesture of supreme knowledge), whose form is synonymous with Īśa (the Lord), who are joyfully sung by the Upaniṣads (the highest limb of the Vedas), and who grant bliss to those who take refuge — I offer salutations.
contentBlocks.meanings: cid-ānanda-rūpa: of the nature of consciousness-bliss | cin-mudrā: the gesture of supreme knowledge (forefinger touching thumb) | udyat-kara: upraised hand | īśa-paryāya-rūpa: of the form that is synonymous with Īśa/Śiva | veda-uttama-aṅga: the highest limb of the Vedas (Upaniṣads) | gīyamāna: being sung/praised | śrita-ānanda-da: giver of bliss to those who take refuge

contentBlocks.original:
जटाजूटमध्ये पुरा या सुराणां
धुनी साद्य कर्मन्दिरूपस्य शम्भोः
गले मल्लिकामालिकाव्याजतस्ते
विभातीति मन्ये गुरो किं तथैव ॥3॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaṭājūṭamadhyē purā yā surāṇāṃ
dhunī sādya karmandirūpasya śambhōḥ
galē mallikāmālikāvyājatastē
vibhātīti manyē gurō kiṃ tathaiva ॥3॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the midst of your matted hair-knot (jaṭā-jūṭa), long ago dwelt the divine river of the gods. Now, in your form as a kamaṇḍalu-bearing Śambhu (holy ascetic), we perceive that very same Gaṅgā appearing at your neck — cleverly disguised as a garland of white jasmine flowers.
contentBlocks.meanings: jaṭā-jūṭa: matted hair-knot | surāṇāṃ dhunī: river of the gods (Gaṅgā) | kamaṇḍi-rūpa: form of a kamaṇḍalu-bearing ascetic | śambhu: auspicious one (Śiva/Śaṅkarācārya) | gala: throat/neck | mallikā-mālikā: garland of jasmine flowers | vyājatas: in disguise/appearance | vibhāti: shines/appears | manyāmahe: we believe/perceive

contentBlocks.original:
नखेन्दुप्रभाधूतनम्रालिहार्दा-
न्धकारव्रजायाब्जमन्दस्मिताय ।
महामोहपाथोनिधेर्बाडबाय
प्रशान्ताय कुर्मो नमः शङ्कराय ॥4॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nakhēnduprabhādhūtanamrālihārdā-
ndhakāravrajāyābjamandasmitāya ।
mahāmōhapāthōnidhērbāḍabāya
praśāntāya kurmō namaḥ śaṅkarāya ॥4॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the supremely peaceful one — whose moon-radiance from his toenails drives away the darkness in the hearts of bowing devotees, whose smile is gentle as a lotus, who is like the submarine volcanic fire (bāḍava-agni) for the deep ocean of great delusion — yet remains perfectly, supremely peaceful (praśānta).
contentBlocks.meanings: nakha-indu-prabhā: moon-radiance of the toenails | dhūta: driven away | namra-āli: bowing ones | hārdā-ndhakāra: darkness of the heart | abja-manda-smita: gentle lotus-smile | mahā-moha-pātho-nidhi: ocean of great delusion | bāḍaba: submarine fire (volcanic fire under the ocean) | praśānta: supremely peaceful | namas te: salutations to you

contentBlocks.original:
प्रणम्रान्तरङ्गाब्जबोधप्रदात्रे
दिवारात्रमव्याहतोस्राय कामम् ।
क्षपेशाय चित्राय लक्ष्म क्षयाभ्यां
विहीनाय कुर्मो नमः शङ्कराय ॥5॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
praṇamrāntaraṅgābjabōdhapradātrē
divārātramavyāhatōsrāya kāmam ।
kṣapēśāya chitrāya lakṣma kṣayābhyāṃ
vihīnāya kurmō namaḥ śaṅkarāya ॥5॥
contentBlocks.translation: To you — who give awakening to the inner lotus-hearts of bowing devotees, who shine uninterruptedly like the sun both day and night, who are also lord of the night (moon) — yet, wonderfully, are free from the defects of waxing and waning — salutations.
contentBlocks.meanings: praṇamra-antaṛaṅga-abja: inner lotus-heart of bowing ones | bodha-pradātṛ: giver of awakening | divā-rātram: day and night | avyāhata-usra: uninterrupted sun | kṣapeśa: lord of the night (moon) | citra: wonderful/strange | lakṣma-kṣayābhyāṃ vihīna: free from the waxing and waning (marks of the moon)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रणम्रास्यपाथोजमोदप्रदात्रे
सदान्तस्तमस्तोमसंहारकर्त्रे ।
रजन्या मपीद्धप्रकाशाय कुर्मो
ह्यपूर्वाय पूष्णे नमः शङ्कराय ॥6॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
praṇamrāsyapāthōjamōdapradātrē
sadāntastamastōmasaṃhārakartrē ।
rajanyā mapīddhaprakāśāya kurmō
hyapūrvāya pūṣṇē namaḥ śaṅkarāya ॥6॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the unprecedented one of complete splendor — who grants joy to the lotus-faces of those who bow before him, who is the eternal destroyer of the masses of inner darkness, and who blazes with light even in the dark of night — the utterly unique, perfectly full one.
contentBlocks.meanings: praṇamra-āsya-pātho-ja: lotus-face of bowing ones | moda-pradātṛ: giver of joy | sadā-antaḥ-tamas-toma: eternal mass of inner darkness | saṃhāra-kartṛ: destroyer | rajanyā api: even in the night | īddha-prakāśa: blazing light | apūrva: unprecedented/never-before-seen | pūrṇa-śrī: of complete splendor

contentBlocks.original:
नतानां हृदब्जानि फुल्लानि शीघ्रं
करोम्याशु योगप्रदानेन नूनम् ।
प्रबोधाय चेत्थं सरोजानि धत्से
प्रफुल्लानि किं भो गुरो ब्रूहि मह्यम् ॥7॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
natānāṃ hṛdabjāni phullāni śīghraṃ
karōmyāśu yōgapradānēna nūnam ।
prabōdhāya chētthaṃ sarōjāni dhatsē
praphullāni kiṃ bhō gurō brūhi mahyam ॥7॥
contentBlocks.translation: 'I quickly cause the lotus-hearts of those who bow to bloom, certainly, by granting yoga,' says the Lord — and indeed, for their awakening, you hold those blossoming lotuses in this manner, filling them with the fragrance of divine knowledge.
contentBlocks.meanings: natānāṃ hṛd-abja: lotus-hearts of bowing ones | phullāni: blossomed/opened | śīghraṃ karomi: I cause quickly | yoga-pradāna: granting of yoga | prabodha: awakening | saroja: lotus | dhatse: you hold | praphulla: fully blossomed

contentBlocks.original:
प्रभाधूतचन्द्रायुतायाखिलेष्ट-
प्रदायानतानां समूहाय शीघ्रम्।
प्रतीपाय नम्रौघदुःखाघपङ्क्ते-
र्मुदा सर्वदा स्यान्नमः शङ्कराय ॥8॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prabhādhūtachandrāyutāyākhilēṣṭa-
pradāyānatānāṃ samūhāya śīghram।
pratīpāya namraughaduḥkhāghapaṅktē-
rmudā sarvadā syānnamaḥ śaṅkarāya ॥8॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you — whose radiance surpasses ten thousand moons, who quickly grants all desires to multitudes of bowing devotees, who stands opposed (as destroyer) to the long line of sorrows and sins of those who bow, and who joyfully grants all knowledge.
contentBlocks.meanings: prabhā-dhūta: luster that surpasses | candra-āyuta: ten thousand moons | akhila-iṣṭa-pradā: granter of all desired things | anatānāṃ samūha: multitude of bowing ones | śīghram: quickly | pratīpa: opposed/adverse/destroyer | namra-augha-duḥkhāgha-paṅkti: long line of sorrows and sins of bowing multitude | mudā: joyfully | sarva-vidyā-pradā: granter of all knowledge

contentBlocks.original:
विनिष्कासितानीश तत्त्वावबोधा -
न्नतानां मनोभ्यो ह्यनन्याश्रयाणि ।
रजांसि प्रपन्नानि पादाम्बुजातं
गुरो रक्तवस्त्रापदेशाद्बिभर्षि ॥9॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viniṣkāsitānīśa tattvāvabōdhā -
nnatānāṃ manōbhyō hyananyāśrayāṇi ।
rajāṃsi prapannāni pādāmbujātaṃ
gurō raktavastrāpadēśādbibharṣi ॥9॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lord, O Guru — the dust-particles (impurities) that have been expelled from the minds of bowing devotees by the knowledge of Truth, having nowhere else to seek refuge, have fallen at your lotus-feet. They have taken sole refuge there, with no other support.
contentBlocks.meanings: viniṣkāsita: expelled/driven out | īśa: O Lord | tattva-avabodha: knowledge of Truth/Reality | natānāṃ manobhyaḥ: from the minds of bowing ones | ananya-āśraya: with no other refuge | rajāṃsi: dust-particles (impurities) | prapannāni: having taken refuge | pāda-ambu-jātaṃ: lotus-feet | guro: O Guru

contentBlocks.original:
मतेर्वेदशीर्षाध्वसम्प्रापकाया-
नतानां जनानां कृपार्द्रैः कटाक्षैः ।
ततेः पापबृन्दस्य शीघ्रं निहन्त्रे
स्मितास्याय कुर्मो नमः शङ्कराय ॥10॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
matērvēdaśīrṣādhvasamprāpakāyā-
natānāṃ janānāṃ kṛpārdraiḥ kaṭākṣaiḥ ।
tatēḥ pāpabṛndasya śīghraṃ nihantrē
smitāsyāya kurmō namaḥ śaṅkarāya ॥10॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who — with his compassion-saturated sidelong glances — leads the minds of bowing seekers along the path to the summit of the Vedas (Vedānta/Upaniṣads), and who is the swift destroyer of multitudes of sins.
contentBlocks.meanings: mati: mind/intellect | veda-śīrṣa-adhva: path to the summit of the Vedas (Vedānta) | samprapaka: one who causes to attain/leads | natānāṃ janānāṃ: bowing people | kṛpā-ārdraiḥ kaṭākṣaiḥ: with compassion-moist sidelong glances | tati: multitude | pāpa-bṛnda: mass of sins | śīghraṃ nihantṛ: swift destroyer

contentBlocks.original:
सुपर्वोक्तिगन्धेन हीनाय तूर्णं
पुरा तोटकायाखिलज्ञानदात्रे।
प्रवालीयगर्वापहारस्य कर्त्रे
पदाब्जम्रदिम्ना नमः शङ्कराय ॥11॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suparvōktigandhēna hīnāya tūrṇaṃ
purā tōṭakāyākhilajñānadātrē।
pravālīyagarvāpahārasya kartrē
padābjamradimnā namaḥ śaṅkarāya ॥11॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the one who — long ago — quickly gave the knowledge of all things to Tōṭaka, who had not even a whiff of skilled speech; who thereby removed the pride of the other learned disciples; and the softness of whose lotus-feet is itself the greatest of all gifts.
contentBlocks.meanings: suparva-ukti-gandha: whiff/scent of skilled speech | hīna: lacking | tūrṇaṃ: quickly | purā: formerly/long ago | toṭakāya: to Tōṭaka | akhila-jñāna-dātṛ: giver of all knowledge | pravālīya-garva: pride of the graduates/learned | apahāra: removal | kartṛ: doer | pada-abja-mardimā: softness of the lotus-feet

contentBlocks.original:
भवाम्भोधिमग्नान्जनान्दुःखयुक्तान्
जवादुद्दिधीर्षुर्भवानित्यहोऽहम् ।
विदित्वा हि ते कीर्तिमन्यादृशाम्भो
सुखं निर्विशङ्कः स्वपिम्यस्तयत्नः ॥12॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavāmbhōdhimagnānjanānduḥkhayuktān
javāduddidhīrṣurbhavānityahō'ham ।
viditvā hi tē kīrtimanyādṛśāmbhō
sukhaṃ nirviśaṅkaḥ svapimyastayatnaḥ ॥12॥
contentBlocks.translation: 'Having learned your fame — that you, in your incomparable compassion, desire to quickly lift up all people submerged in the ocean of existence and burdened with suffering — alas, I too have taken refuge in you.' Thus the devotee surrenders completely.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhava-ambhodhi-magna: submerged in the ocean of existence | duḥkha-yukta: burdened with suffering | javāt-uddidhīrṣu: desiring to lift up quickly | bhavān: you (respectful) | viditvā: having learned/knowing | te kīrtim: your fame | anya-dṛśa-ambho-nidhi: incomparable ocean of [fame/compassion] | śaraṇaṃ gataḥ asmi: I have taken refuge

contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Bhujanga Prayāta Stotram composed by Śrī Śaṅkarācārya.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti: thus ends | śrī śaṅkarācārya: the great Ādi Śaṅkarācārya | bhujanga-prayāta-stotram: stotram in the bhujanga-prayāta meter (the serpent-gliding meter)
